summary: The diff implements all four requirements. REQ-1 is addressed in backend/main.mo with the Google Custom Search API integration and in useQueries.ts with JSON parsing and error surfacing. REQ-2 is addressed in backend/main.mo with HTTPS enforcement and the proxyRequest function, plus ProxyContent.tsx for iframe rendering. REQ-3 is addressed in useQueries.ts mutations and BrowserLayout.tsx wiring loading/error/success states through useTabManager. REQ-4 is addressed in SearchResults.tsx with the onNavigate click handler and BrowserLayout.tsx routing search result clicks through the proxy. The implementation appears largely complete with one notable gap: the Google API key is absent from the search URL construction.

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure the HTTP outcall to the Google Custom Search API is correctly constructed with the API key and Search Engine ID
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo constructs the search URL with only the Search Engine ID (cx) and query (q) parameters but no API key parameter. The googleSearchURL only includes ?q= and &cx= but no &key= parameter, which means the API call will likely be rejected by Google's API.

hallucinated_features.description: Content-Security-Policy header injection via a transform callback that filters X-Cheetah- prefixed headers
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not specify adding CSP headers or filtering custom X-Cheetah- headers in the proxy transform callback. This is extra logic not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Form submission interception inside proxied iframes
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only requested fixing proxy loading and search result link-click navigation. Intercepting form submissions inside the proxied iframe was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Base tag injection and CSP meta tag removal in proxied HTML content
hallucinated_features.reason: Injecting base tags and stripping CSP meta tags from proxied HTML was not explicitly requested in the build requirements.
